title: Authors and Citation
text: Benjamin Becker. Author, maintainer.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Becker B (2026). eatDataTest: educational assessment tools: Data Testing. R package version 0.1.0, https://github.com/beckerbenj/eatDataTest.
code: @Manual{,   title = {eatDataTest: educational assessment tools: Data Testing},   author = {Benjamin Becker},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.1.0},   url = {https://github.com/beckerbenj/eatDataTest}, }

title: educational assessment tools: Data Testing
text: eatDataTest (educational assessment tools: Data Tests) framework testing comparing different data set versions.

title: educational assessment tools: Data Testing
code: # Install development version from GitHub via remotes::install_github("beckerbenj/eatDataTest")

title: educational assessment tools: Data Testing
text: core functionality package currently consists two functions:

title: Add Data. — add_data
text: Add infrastructure new data set eatDataTest repository. includes .yaml file markdown changelog (.md file) test file (.R file tests) initial test result icon (.svg file tests) initial version badge (.svg file tests) snippet can inserted Readme.md.

title: Add Data. — add_data
code: add_data(   .path = getwd(),   name,   release_path,   oldrel_path = NULL,   version = "v1.0",   depends = NULL )

title: Add Data. — add_data
text: .path Path eatDataTest repository. Defaults current working directory. name Name data set. files named accordingly. release_path Path data set. oldrel_path Optional: Path previous data version. version Version number current data set. Defaults 'v1.0'. depends data sets data set depend ? List data set names separated ", ".

title: Add Data. — add_data
text: Creates data set infrastructure. Returns markdown snippet.

what: Compare meta data where possible. — compare_meta_data_where_possible
title: Compare meta data where possible. — compare_meta_data_where_possible
text: function allows comparing meta data two data sets variables identical names data sets.

title: Compare meta data where possible. — compare_meta_data_where_possible
Compare meta data where possible. — compare_meta_data_where_possible
text: data1 First GADSdat object. data2 Second GADSdat object. suffix Suffix removed

title: Compare meta data where possible. — compare_meta_data_where_possible
text: Returns named list via eatFDZ::compare_data(): compared_variables: variables compared? differences_variable_labels: Differences variable labels. differences_value_labels: Differences value labels missing tags.

title: Compare meta data where possible. — compare_meta_data_where_possible
text: compare_meta_data_where_possible() used within data tests, highly recommended test compared_variables ensure tests actually passing just lacking.

title: Compare meta data where possible. — compare_meta_data_where_possible
Create Data Yaml. — create_data_yaml
text: Create data yaml contains paths current data version ('release_path'), previous version ('oldrel_path') current version number ('version').

title: Create Data Yaml. — create_data_yaml
code: create_data_yaml(   .path = getwd(),   name,   release_path,   oldrel_path = NULL,   version = "v1.0",   depends = NULL )

title: Create Data Yaml. — create_data_yaml
text: .path Path eatDaT repository. Defaults current working directory. name Name data set. file named accordingly. release_path Path data set. oldrel_path Optional: Path previous data version. Defaults NULL. version Version number current data set. Defaults 'v1.0'. depends data sets data set depend ? List data set names separated ", ".

title: Create Data Yaml. — create_data_yaml
text: Creates .yaml. Returns NULL.

what: Create Data and Meta Data Diff. — create_diff
title: Create Data and Meta Data Diff. — create_diff
text: create_meta_diff creates list differing variables well lists meta differences variable value level. create_data_diff create list differing variables cross tables differing variable. create_diff calls functions.

title: Create Data and Meta Data Diff. — create_diff
code: create_diff(.path = getwd(), name, id, tolerance = sqrt(.Machine$double.eps))  create_meta_diff(.path = getwd(), name)  create_data_diff(   .path = getwd(),   name,   id,   tolerance = sqrt(.Machine$double.eps) )

title: Create Data and Meta Data Diff. — create_diff
text: .path Path eatDataTest repository. Defaults current working directory. name Name data set. file named accordingly. id Name id variable(s) data set. tolerance numeric value greater equal 0. Differences smaller tolerance reported. default value close 1.5e-8.

title: Create Data and Meta Data Diff. — create_diff
text: Creates two .xlsx files. Returns NULL.

title: Create Data and Meta Data Diff. — create_diff
Import Data. — import_data
text: Imports data set based yaml file. Currently, import_data() supports .RDS .sav files.

title: Import Data. — import_data
text: .path Path eatDataTest repository. Defaults current working directory. name Name data set. file named accordingly. data_version Available options 'release' 'oldrel'. Defaults 'release'.

title: Import Data. — import_data
text: Returns data set GADSdat format.

title: Read Data Yaml. — read_data_yaml
text: read_data_yaml() reads data yaml created via create_data_yaml(). respective get-functions wrappers around read_data_yaml() simply return single character value.

title: Read Data Yaml. — read_data_yaml
code: read_data_yaml(.path = getwd(), name, absolute_paths = TRUE)  get_release_path(.path = getwd(), name)  get_oldrel_path(.path = getwd(), name)  get_version(.path = getwd(), name)  get_depends(.path = getwd(), name)

title: Read Data Yaml. — read_data_yaml
text: .path Path eatDataTest repository. Defaults current working directory. name Name data set. file named accordingly. absolute_paths paths release oldrel converted absolute paths?

title: Read Data Yaml. — read_data_yaml
text: Returns named list following entries: version: Version number current data set. release: Path current data version. oldrel: Path previous data version. depends: data sets data set depend ? List data set names separated ", ".

title: Run Data Tests. — run_data_tests
text: Run tests data set.

title: Run Data Tests. — run_data_tests
code: run_data_tests(   .path = getwd(),   name,   overwrite_icon = TRUE,   overwrite_version = TRUE )

title: Run Data Tests. — run_data_tests
text: .path Path eatDataTest repository. Defaults current working directory. name Name data set. overwrite_icon result icon tests folder overwritten? overwrite_version version number tests folder overwritten?

title: Run Data Tests. — run_data_tests
text: Creates test result icon. Returns NULL.

title: Run Reverse Depedency Tests. — run_revdep_tests
text: Run reverse dependency tests data set.

title: Run Reverse Depedency Tests. — run_revdep_tests
text: .path Path eatDataTest repository. Defaults current working directory. name Name data set. overwrite_icons test result icons reverse dependenies overwritten?.

title: Run Reverse Depedency Tests. — run_revdep_tests
text: Returns test report.

title: Run Reverse Depedency Tests. — run_revdep_tests
text: project contains multiple data sets, can sometimes depend . example, multiple imputaions data may depend unimputed data set. run_revdep_tests() borrows idea reverse dependency tests R package revdep applies data sets. instance, project contains three data sets (, B, C). data sets B C depend data set . run_revdep_tests() run data set , data tests performed data sets B C.

title: Setup eatDataTest. — setup_eatDataTest
text: Add infrastructure using directory aeatDataTest repository. includes data folder changelogs folder tests folder diff folder Readme.md containing following elements: table list data sets instructions use repository data contributers.

title: Setup eatDataTest. — setup_eatDataTest
text: .path Path eatDataTest repository. Defaults current working directory.

title: Setup eatDataTest. — setup_eatDataTest
text: Creates repository infrastructure. Returns markdown snippet.

title: eatDataTest 0.1.0
text: Initial Github release
